evals.prompt: Analyze the transactions in evals/files/sample_transactions.csv. Give me a summary with total income, total expenses, spending by category, and monthly cash flow.
evals.expected_output: A structured analysis showing income totals, expense totals, category breakdowns, and net cash flow
expectations: Total income is correctly calculated as $10,500.00 (5500 + 3200 + 1800)
Total expenses are correctly calculated (sum of all negative amounts)
Spending is broken down by category with at least 5 categories shown
Net cash flow / surplus is calculated (income minus expenses)
Uses pandas for the analysis rather than manual calculation

evals.prompt: Look at evals/files/sample_transactions.csv and tell me which spending categories are the highest, and flag any unusual transactions. Also identify how spending splits between my two accounts.
evals.expected_output: Category ranking by spend amount, flagged outliers, and per-account spending breakdown
expectations: Housing is identified as the largest expense category ($2,200)
Spending is broken down by account (Chase Checking vs Amex Gold)
At least one transaction is flagged as notable or unusual
Categories are ranked or sorted by total spend amount
Missing values or data quality issues are mentioned (even if none found)
